entries.value: Carbon’s search component provides the basic input mechanism for conducting a search. No accessibility annotations are needed for the input, but design annotations are needed to make the search messaging and results accessible.
Search suggestions and typeaheads
Users should be able to TAB into the input field of the search box to begin typing and press ENTER to run the search query.

For type-ahead suggestions, the ARROW keys should cycle through displayed suggestions, with ENTER choosing a suggestion and ESCAPE allowing the user to exit the type-ahead menu without selecting anything.
Search outcomes and status (e.g., “25 results found”, “no results found”)
Results navigation
In Material Design 2, there is no search component - all information is located under the "Text fields" (input) section.
Use at a global level when searching an entire site.
Carbon provides a search pattern that outlines three search types: basic, active, and focused
Avoid dead ends
Include a loading indicator
Display the number of results
Don’t include a label
If a search returns “No results”, suggest a follow-up action. Provide suggestions and helpful resources to aid the user in finding what they are looking for. For more on no results for searches

If the search will take longer than a moment or two, include a loading indicator. Your loading state should reflect your empty state with useful helper text signaling that the search is still ongoing. Include a progress bar for advanced, resource-intensive searches. Let the user know how far along the search is, and roughly how long it will take to finish.
Always include the number of search results, including for searches with no results. If you plan to offer a scope filter, also include the number of results for each scope selector.

Avoid adding a label to your search field. Users expect and understand search fields, and no label is necessary. A search icon along with useful placeholder text should clearly indicate that the field is intended for search.
Search offers users a way to explore a website or application using keywords. Search can be used as the primary means of discovering content or as a filter to aid the user in finding content.
there is no search component - all information is located under the "Text fields" (input) section.
Search enables users to specify a word or a phrase to find relevant content without navigation
A search field is used for searching and filtering items.
Provide useful placeholder text and include a search icon.
Localize the search field-For languages that read right to left, flip the layout of the search box as shown in the next example

Add a scope filter to your search field- The optional scoped search allows users to limit their searches to a section or category of content on a website, as opposed to a global search that searches everything at once. Only one scoped category can be selected at a time, but the dropdown should always include an “All” or “Any” option, and this option should be selected by default. With “All” or “Any” selected, the field will function as
Show a "No results" state instead of an error
Any search field should include a label inside the field, in the default state.
The label within the search field should be in sentence case
Don’t include a label for search fields
The search icon is recognized and acceptable across languages and countries
